title: Null (species)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Null}} {{Species |image=[[File:Towering_Null.png]] |name=Null |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]'' |class=[[Near-Human]] |subspecies= |races= |height=Up to 2.88 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=Tall stature, heavy beards (in [[Gender/Legends|males]]) |lifespan= |origin=[[Null/Legends|Null]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Always glad to welcome our hunters to the inn. As you can see, we have important guests, very important guests. But we do not neglect our regular trade.|A Null innkeeper to Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker|}} The '''Nulls''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tall [[Near-Human]]s who inhabited the eponymous [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Null/Legends|Null]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. A fierce and individualistic people, they had a culture of [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] and stonemasons, and they based their planetary economy on hunting tourism. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], when the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], their planet seceded to the Confederacy, although much of the populace had a great respect for the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], the devoted protectors of the Republic. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], a Null [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agent was employed by the [[Church of the Dark Side]] as an imitator of the [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Emperor's Mage]] [[Jedgar]], an imposingly tall [[Human/Legends|Human]] who matched Nulls in height. ==Biology and appearance== [[File:Jedgar-PotDS.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Headshot of a male Null]] The Nulls closely resembled baseline [[Human/Legends|Humans]], with four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], a bipedal stance and a relatively [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] body.''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]'' However, the Nulls could be easily identified by their towering stature. Some individuals were known to be a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] taller than the Human [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], whose respective adult heights were 1.88{{SWE|characters|anakinskywalker|Anakin Skywalker}} and 1.82 meters.{{SWE|characters|obiwankenobi|Obi-Wan Kenobi}} Humans with [[Giantism|abnormally high linear growth]] could easily pass as Nulls, and vice-versa.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} Like Humans, the Nulls had [[Hair/Legends|hair]] covering their scalp. Male members of the species also grew long and heavy beards on their chins, which they usually braided for aesthetical reasons. At least one [[Heingort Giddis|male individual]], a professional impersonator, was known to have light skin, a prominent chin, hooded [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a large, hooked [[Nose/Legends|nose]]. That one Null also had his hands roughly the size of a grown Human's head. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|I see you are admiring the stonework of the inn. It is a native art. One pull of the keystone and the whole wall comes tumbling down.|The Null innkeeper|}} On their eponymous [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the Nulls lived in small individualistic communities with a strong sense of privacy. The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] had no large cities, and although there existed a planetary government with its system of laws, criminality was normally handled among villagers through fierce retaliation. The Null villages had narrow streets, cluttered with low buildings made of [[Stone/Legends|stone]] and [[Wood/Legends|wood]]. At the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the villagers would seldom use [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]] and usually relied on the [[bellock]]s for transportation. Individuals of both [[Gender/Legends|sexes]] [[Clothing/Legends|dressed]] in thigh-length [[Boot/Legends|boots]] and animal skins. [[Hunting/Legends|Hunting]] was an important activity on Null, since the planet's mountains were full of wild beasts prized for their skins—such as the wily [[laroon]]. Despite their fierce nature and their keen sense of privacy, the Nulls normally displayed a friendly demeanour towards strangers, especially since their planet's economy relied on hunting tourism. Although their habitations could be considered archaic by [[Technology level|galactic standards]], the Nulls were in fact master stoneworkers. They built walls from jagged stones, fitting them together in complex patterns, but without mortar or joinery to bind them. More than mere architecture, this specific knowledge was considered an art form. ==History== {{Quote|Dooku has proposed this as a friendly meeting at his villa on the world of Null.''"
"''I've heard of this world. Dooku has its leader in his pocket. It was one of the first to join the Separatists.|[[Lorian Nod]] and Obi-Wan Kenobi|}} The [[Near-Human]]s known as the Nulls hailed from the [[Tree/Legends|forested]] planet of the [[Null/Legends|same name]], located within the [[Trans-Vulta sector/Legends|Trans-Vulta]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]], in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The Nulls' world was first discovered by outside observers between [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000]] and [[3000 BBY]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' On the fifth [[Standard month/Legends|month]]{{InsiderCite|84|Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241}} of the year [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], a galaxy-wide conflict know as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Since [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]—former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and head of the Confederacy—almost wholly owned the planet[[Republic 65|''Republic'' 65]] and had ties with its leaders, Null quickly sided with the Confederacy. However, the common people went on with welcoming tourists from other worlds. [[19 BBY/Legends|Three years]] later, the Clone Wars were brought to an end, and the Republic was replaced by the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], an authoritarian government led by former [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].{{Film|III}} ==Nulls in the galaxy== [[File:Jedgar_and_Hissa-PotDS.jpg|thumb|200px|The false Jedgar and the Grand Moff Hissa]] {{Quote|Trioculus, Grand Moff Hissa, I trust that our worthy visitors suffered no ill effects from gamma radiation when you reached the Null Zone.|The false Jedgar greets [[Trioculus]] and [[Bertroff Hissa]]|}} In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Empire was defeated at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. The former [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] of the [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Cronal]] helped acting-[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] set up a mock religion known as the "[[Church of the Dark Side]]" on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], establishing a body of false [[Prophets of the Dark Side]]. Cronal recruited a Null Imperial agent named Heingort Giddis, instructing him to impersonate [[Jedgar]], the real [[High Prophet]] of the dark side, who was believed dead. The impostor served along with [[Rajah Ubooki]], a [[Bimm/Legends|Bimm]] impersonator posing as the [[Supreme Prophet]] [[Kadann]], and they sought to seize the secrets of an ancient [[Jedi Library]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. However, the so-called Prophets were defeated by the heroes of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]],[[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']] and were eventually [[Death/Legends|murdered]] by the Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Afsheen Makati]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Nulls first appeared in ''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]'', a young readers novel written by [[Jude Watson]] and published by [[Scholastic]] on [[August 1]], [[2003]]. In early [[June]] [[2006]], ''Star Wars'' author [[Abel G. Peña]] wrote ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]'', an online supplement to the [[Star Wars Insider 88|eighty-eighth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine. In this article, Peña identified a character from the 1990s [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|''Jedi Prince'']] series as a Null impostor, thus [[Retroactive continuity|retconning]] the species's first appearance to [[Paul Davids|Paul]] and [[Hollace Davids]]' novel ''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]'', published on [[June 1]], [[1992]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]'' {{C|First appearance before retcon}} *''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]'' {{1st}} {{Ret}} *''[[Mission from Mount Yoda]]'' {{Ret}} *[[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']] {{Ret}} ==Sources== *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} *{{CSWECite|II|394|Null}} *{{SW|url=news/the-star-wars-spy-game-spin-declassified|text=The ''Star Wars'' Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|int=The Star Wars Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Human and Near-Human species]] [[Category:Mid Rim sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Nulls]] }}
